subject: Re: Dual boot system for Warp/Relion
body:
Hi Przemysław,

I set up a 2 GPU dual-boot system in my postdoc lab. It worked okay, but it
tended to overheat quite a bit, and I ended up not being able to process
our most recent tomography data because it didn't have enough memory
available to Warp. I set this up sometime in ~2018 or so, so it is kind of
ancient by today's standards. The way we set it up was that one GPU
belonged to the Linux system, and the other GPU could switch between Linux
and Windows so we could have 2 GPUs available to process data in Relion. It
was definitely a lot slower than our cluster, and it actually ended up
crashing a few times because the GPUs eventually got too hot while data was
processing. All in alll, I wouldn't recommend setting up a dual-boot
system, but those wiser than me in terms of setting up computers can also
chime in :)

Thanks,
Jess

On Sat, Feb 26, 2022 at 12:03 AM Przemysław Dutka <
przemyslaw.dutka@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi everyone,
>
> I'm trying to upgrade our hardware setup for relion/warp/m data
> processing. I have no experience with that setup. Is anyone using a dual
> boot system for relion/warp? Any thoughts and/or advice?
>
> Thanks,
> Przemek
>
